paper_title: Material and Methods of Meiofaunal and Sediment Analysis in the Gulf of Pozzuoli

methods.description: Collection of sediment samples from the Gulf of Pozzuoli using a Van Veen grab to analyze meiofaunal assemblages and sediment characteristics.

steps.description: Eleven samples of very fine to coarse sands, and very fine gravels, were collected by a Van Veen grab above the shelf break of the Gulf of Pozzuoli.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using Van Veen grab

steps.description: The grab collected superficial sediments, including the first ca. 5 cm of the seabed.
steps.searchable_summary: extract top 5 cm of seabed sediment

methods.description: Analysis of meiofaunal assemblages in sediment samples to determine species diversity and abundance.

steps.description: All the samples were oven-dried, and 100 g of dry sediment was taken.
steps.searchable_summary: oven-dry sediment samples and weigh 100 g

steps.description: They were washed through 230-mesh (63 lm) and 120-mesh (125 lm) sieves, and the residues were oven-dried and examined with a reflected light microscope.
Q-mode cluster analysis was performed to determine groups of samples with similar meiofaunal composition.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Q-mode cluster analysis on meiofaunal data

steps.description: Pearson's correlation coefficient was used to test for correlation between assemblage features, depth, major and trace elements, and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons.

methods.description: Analysis of sediment grain size following the standard methodology of Folk and Ward (1957).

steps.description: Grain size analyses were performed following the standard methodology of Folk and Ward (1957).
steps.searchable_summary: perform grain size analysis using Folk and Ward method

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data, including diversity indices and correlation analysis.

steps.description: Computation of diversity indices and statistical analysis were performed with STATISTICA 5.
Pearson's correlation coefficient analysis was performed on the eight fine-grained samples included in Cluster A.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Pearson's correlation on fine-grained samples
